definition: Geopolitical supply fragmentation embeds a structural inflation premium through four documented channels that central banks cannot offset without recession: energy supply fragmentation (European gas 2-3x pre-war), trade route disruption (Red Sea +$2K-$4K/container), reshoring costs (semiconductors 30-50% above Asian production), and commodity weaponization.
evidence: Red Sea disruption adding $2K-$4K per container
Semiconductor reshoring costs 30-50% above Asian production
Debate pair_3 confirmed channel identification is 'rigorous'
open_questions: Do AI productivity gains and China overcapacity offset the fragmentation premium?
How much of the supply fragmentation cost is already absorbed into the price level vs creating ongoing inflationary pressure?
Is the correlation-relevant question incremental new shocks, not backward-looking absorbed costs?
